resolvedQuestions.question: Which motion improvements remain after 009 was implemented?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Do not re-add the restraint gate or cards. Residual value is advanced craft top-up, resource traceability, fixture consistency, and an on-demand advanced rendering boundary.

resolvedQuestions.question: Are there router/resource efficiency gaps?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Yes. The loading table says shared register and decision framework are ALWAYS resources, but the pseudocode default only loads corpus_map and guards paths inside the packet.

resolvedQuestions.question: Does the playbook validate the right behavior?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Mostly, but expected_resources drift from the ALWAYS contract and it lacks a hub-level mode-routing benchmark scenario.

resolvedQuestions.question: Which external ideas still add usefulness?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Popover transform-origin, tooltip instant follow-up, @starting-style, slow-motion debugging, Framer Motion shorthand caveat, and propose-first View Transition/advanced rendering guidance.

resolvedQuestions.question: What should be avoided?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Avoid bulk import, broad router overfitting, duplicate completed 009 assets, motion-rich defaults, GSAP/scroll mandates, and motion-performance release scoring inside motion.

keyFindings.summary: Align the motion router resource-loading contract with the prose ALWAYS table.
The 009 must-add motion gate and three card assets are already implemented.
A small advanced-craft top-up has higher leverage than a broad advanced rendering reference.
Manual playbook expected resources omit mandatory resources in several scenarios.

The local benchmark evidence does not include a persisted motion Mode A report, so routing confidence should be preserved with a motion-specific artifact or fixture rather than inferred from interface.
Motion release docs still mention old flat-skill artifacts not present in the nested packet structure.
Advanced rendering should remain on-demand and propose-first, not a default motion path.
Motion handoff should preserve the existing animation system and record mechanism choice before sk-code implementation.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Rebuild the 009 motion gate and cards

ruledOutDirections.reason: Prior 009 synthesis explicitly selected operational cards and checklists over corpus volume.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Make motion-performance review a motion-mode responsibility
ruledOutDirections.reason: Motion owns build guidance; audit owns findings-first release scoring.
Taxonomy is settled around the parent hub and five modes.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat all interfaces as motion-rich by default
ruledOutDirections.reason: The register and restraint gate distinguish Product from Brand and trim high-frequency motion.
